title: Eriocoma wallowaensis
intro: Eriocoma wallowaensis, also known as Wallowa beardtongue, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that may aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. Bioactive constituents of Eriocoma wallowaensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Eriocoma wallowaensis include teas and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eriocoma wallowaensis is classified as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Poaceae, which includes grasses. Further classification places it within the subfamily Bambusoideae, and the tribe Bambuseae. This plant is native to North America and is often found in mountainous regions. Its classification also suggests a close relation to other species within the same tribe and subfamily.
